signature: Deterministic "multiply by a small rational and round" dynamics whose symbolic itinerary (parity/digit/carry stream) must satisfy an exact side condition FOREVER, against a measure-theoretic drift that makes eternal satisfaction have heuristic probability zero-or-golden-ratio; and finite certificates (regular languages, local rules, bounded windows, linear/sparse spaces) that would verify such an eternal condition from a finite check.

finds.ref: Kurt Mahler, "An unsolved problem on the powers of 3/2", 1968, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 8, 313-321 (repr. Documenta Math., Mahler Selecta 2019)
finds.the_theorem: Define a Z-number as x>0 with {x(3/2)^n} in [0,1/2) for ALL n>=0. Mahler proves: (i) writing x(3/2)^n = g_n + r_n, the integer parts obey g_{n+1} = (3g_n + eps_n)/2 where eps_n = parity(g_n), i.e. g_{n+1} = ceil(3g_n/2), and case B (g_n odd) forces r_n in [1/3,1/2), case A forces r_n in [0,1/3); (ii) the SAME series sum eps_{m+k}(3/2)^k converges in R to 3r_m and 2-adically to -3g_m; (iii) the parity word eps contains no factor "11" (his ineq. (13)); (iv) the number T(t) of residue classes mod 2^t that can contain the odd integer part G_1 of a Z-number satisfies T(t)+1 = Fibonacci(t+1) (generating function 1/(1-z-z^2)), hence at most ~x^{log2 phi} ≈ x^0.7 Z-numbers up to x, so at most countably many, at most one per unit interval; (v) an explicit break-off algorithm: G_{k+1} = (3/2)^{a_k} H_k with H_k=(3G_k+1)/2 and 2^{a_k}||H_k, which terminates ('no Z-number here') the moment an exact 2-adic divisibility fails — no seed is known for which it runs forever.
finds.the_match: Nearly exact. Mahler's integer map g -> ceil(3g/2) (3g/2 if even, (3g+1)/2 if odd) is the mirror conjugate (via n -> -n) of Antihydra/Hydra's n -> floor(3n/2); the parity word is the same symbolic itinerary; 'algorithm runs forever iff an exact side condition on the itinerary holds forever against drift' is exactly the cryptid halting shape, and his G_k/H_k/a_k cascade interleaves x3/2 with exact v2-valuation extraction — the Space Needle's value+v2 dynamics hitting the boundary 'H_k odd'. Quantitative echo: Mahler's Fibonacci count of surviving residue classes mod 2^t vs Antihydra's heuristic halting probability (1/phi)^n — the same algebraic number phi, BUT derived differently (no-11 transfer matrix from the r_n<1/2 window vs ruin probability of the +2/-1 counter walk, root of p^2+p-1); a bijection between the two word classes is plausible but NOT established. Second gap: Mahler's eternal condition is a pointwise window on r_n (locally checkable on the itinerary), while Antihydra's is a running-sum boundary (a non-local functional) — Mahler's counting adapts to finite horizons but not verbatim. Blindness verified: full text has zero mentions of Collatz/3x+1 (paper predates the literature; poses the problem from 'a Japanese colleague').
finds.transfer: (1) Rigorous finite-horizon survivor-density theorem for Antihydra: parity itinerary of t steps determines the seed mod 2^t (Terras-style injectivity holds for floor(3n/2)); count itinerary words of length t whose +2/-1 counter never hits the boundary via the exact ballot-type transfer matrix, concluding #{seeds <= x surviving t steps} <= C x (lambda/2)^t with lambda the walk's survival growth rate — converting the (1/phi)^n heuristic into a density theorem, in exact parallel to Mahler's T(t)+1=F(t+1). (2) Port Mahler's dual real/2-adic convergence: the parity stream of any non-halting cryptid orbit simultaneously encodes a real number (fractional data, constrained to a window) and a 2-adic integer (the seed) — i.e., every non-halting Antihydra seed induces a Mahler-type real number for the map x3/2 with a modified window; make the reduction exact and cryptid non-halting becomes a Z-number-existence variant. (3) Import Mahler's break-off algorithm as a Space-Needle normal form.

finds.ref: Yann Bugeaud, Arturas Dubickas, "Fractional parts of powers and Sturmian words", 2005, C. R. Acad. Sci. Paris Ser. I 341, 69-74; with L. Flatto, J. Lagarias, A. Pollington 1995 (Acta Arith.) as input
finds.the_theorem: Thm 0.2: for integer b>=2 and irrational xi>0, the fractional parts {xi b^n} (n>=0) cannot all lie in an interval of length < 1/b; and they all lie in a closed interval of length exactly 1/b IFF xi = g + k/(b-1) + t_b(w) where w is a STURMIAN word on {0,1} (t_b = value of w read base-b); any such xi is transcendental. Input theorem (Flatto-Lagarias-Pollington 1995): for coprime p>q>=2, no xi>0 has all {xi(p/q)^n} in an interval of length < 1/p; Dubickas extends to algebraic alpha that are neither Pisot nor Salem, with the sharp length 1/l(P^{r+1}) (reduced length of the minimal polynomial).
finds.the_match: Same object as the Mahler find one level up: 'orbit of x(rational)^n confined to a window forever' with the EXTREMAL solutions exactly characterized as Sturmian itineraries — for our program, the extremal/tightest-constrained parity words (e.g. hypothetical Collatz cycle parity vectors, which Halbeisen-Hungerbuhler-type optimality forces to be balanced/Christoffel-like of slope tied to log2 3) are precisely Sturmian-type words. Gaps: their sharp characterization is for INTEGER base b (the multiply map with no carry interaction); for p/q=3/2 the analogous characterization is open (only the 1/p lower bound is known), and Collatz adds the +1 carry which has no counterpart in xi(3/2)^n. Blindness: full text checked, zero Collatz/3x+1 mentions (cites Mahler, FLP, Weyl).
finds.transfer: Prove or test: the length-maximal Antihydra survivor words of each length converge to a Sturmian word of the appropriate slope (extremal itineraries are Sturmian); concretely, run the survivor enumeration from our Antihydra experiments and check balancedness/factor-complexity n+1 of the lexicographic-extremal surviving parity words, then attempt a Bugeaud-Dubickas-style proof that any INFINITE survivor word must contain the forbidden unbalanced factors unless it is Sturmian — reducing 'Antihydra never halts for seed n' to a Sturmian rigidity statement for which their combinatorics-on-words machinery (and Dubickas' interval arithmetic on the series sum eps_k (q/p)^k) transfers directly.

finds.ref: Richard Guy, Cedric A.B. Smith, "The G-values of various games", 1956, Proc. Cambridge Phil. Soc. 52, 514-526 (Guy-Smith periodicity theorem); Anil Gangolli, Thane Plambeck, "A note on periodicity in some octal games", 1989, Int. J. Game Theory 18, 311-320 (Berlekamp sparse-space method)
finds.the_theorem: Guy-Smith periodicity theorem: for an octal game whose code has last nonzero digit at position k, if the Grundy sequence satisfies G(n+p) = G(n) for every n with n0 <= n < 2n0 + p + k, then G(n+p) = G(n) for ALL n >= n0 — a finite window check certifies an exact condition forever (hypothesis that matters: G(n) is computed by mex over moves that split a heap using only G-values of SMALLER heaps with bounded-offset structure). Sparse-space method (Berlekamp; Gangolli-Plambeck 1989): exhibit a small GF(2)-linear 'sparse space' S (closed under XOR) plus coset data such that all computed Grundy values lie in S and the mex-recursion provably keeps them there; used to settle periodicity of .16 (period 149459, preperiod 105350), .56, .127, .376. Open: whether ALL finite octal games have eventually periodic Grundy sequences (e.g. .007 = Grundy's game, open since 1939).
finds.the_match: The octal-game periodicity family is a sibling open-problem family with our smell inverted: an infinite exact pattern condition (eventual periodicity of a deterministically generated integer sequence) that resists proof game-by-game, attacked via finite certificates. Two structural matches: (a) Guy-Smith is a 'finite check implies forever' theorem — the exact shape a Collatz/cryptid certificate theorem must have; its load-bearing hypothesis (bounded lookback: G(n) depends on G at boundedly-indexed smaller arguments) FAILS for Collatz (multiplicative recursion, unbounded lookback) — this is the precise gap; (b) sparse spaces are XOR-closed invariant-set certificates found by finite search and propagated by algebraic closure — a certificate class DISJOINT from our regular-language class (a GF(2)-linear constraint on binary residues is automatic-like but its DFA can be exponentially large, so our <=7-state negative result says nothing about it). Blindness: Crossref reference list of Gangolli-Plambeck checked — 10 refs, all game theory except Guy's 'Unsolved Problems in Number Theory' (1981) cited at p.127, which I could not inspect (UPINT does contain 3x+1 as problem E16, so there is an unresolved chance that one citation points at the Collatz pages); the Guy-Smith 1956 theorem itself is certainly blind.
finds.transfer: Widen our divergence/cryptid certificate search from regular languages to sparse-space-style certificates: seek a GF(2)-affine family S_t of parity/residue constraints (XOR-closed sets of (n mod 2^k, counter mod m) states) invariant under the Antihydra step with the halt condition excluded, verified by finite linear algebra and propagated by closure — i.e., replay Gangolli-Plambeck with mex/XOR replaced by the floor(3n/2) transition. Second transfer: formulate the sharpest possible Guy-Smith analogue for residue-indexed Collatz data (e.g. the sequence sigma(k) = behavior class of n mod 2^k) and identify exactly which bounded-lookback hypothesis fails — a theorem of the form 'no Guy-Smith window theorem exists for T' would itself be a publishable rigidity statement.

finds.ref: Norman Carey, David Clampitt, "Aspects of well-formed scales", 1989, Music Theory Spectrum 11(2), 187-206; generalization: Marek Zabka, J. Math. & Music 4(1), 2010; cf. R. Cubarsi, "An alternative approach to generalized Pythagorean scales", 2020s
finds.the_theorem: Carey-Clampitt theorem: a scale of N notes generated by iterating a single interval theta (for Pythagorean tuning theta = log2 3, i.e. stacking perfect fifths mod octave) is WELL-FORMED (the generator spans a constant number of scale steps; equivalently symmetry=closure; equivalently each generic interval size comes in exactly two specific sizes, the Myhill property) if and only if N is a denominator of a convergent or semiconvergent of the continued fraction of theta. For theta = log2 3 the well-formed cardinalities are exactly 1,2,3,5,7,12,17,29,41,53,... and the scale words are Christoffel/mechanical words of slope theta, organized in a Stern-Brocot hierarchy.
finds.the_match: The quantity governing everything is log2 3 — the SAME constant whose Diophantine properties control Collatz cycle structure: a hypothetical cycle with a odd and b even steps needs 2^{a+b} close to 3^a, i.e. b/a a near-convergent of log2(3/2), and known cycle-length lower bounds (Eliahou 1993-style) enumerate exactly the Carey-Clampitt cardinalities; moreover the optimal cycle parity vectors are balanced words — the scale words themselves. So music theory's classification of 'which stacking depths close up well' IS the skeleton catalog of admissible Collatz cycle shapes, discovered independently and blind. Gaps: this half of the match partially rediscovers what Collatz cycle experts already do with continued fractions (Eliahou, Halbeisen-Hungerbuhler are Collatz-aware), so novelty is NOT the convergents but the extra well-formed structure theory (Myhill two-sizes property, hierarchical Stern-Brocot decomposition of the scale words, the symmetry=closure equivalence) which has no Collatz-side counterpart yet; and it constrains cycles only — it says nothing about divergent orbits, whose parity densities need not be extremal. Blindness: Carey-Clampitt 1989 is a music theory journal (original paywalled; checked the field via Cubarsi's full text and Zabka's abstract — zero Collatz mentions anywhere in this literature).
finds.transfer: Transfer the Myhill/two-sizes theorem to cycle parity words: prove that the parity word of any minimal hypothetical T-cycle, being forced within O(1) discrepancy of a mechanical word of slope log2(3/2) - adjacent rational, must realize each 'generic gap' between consecutive odd steps in exactly two sizes d, d+1 with prescribed multiplicities given by the Stern-Brocot position of a/b — turning the known 'balanced-ish' constraint into an exact finite list of admissible gap-multisets per (a,b), shrinking the cycle search space; then check whether the same two-sizes constraint applies to Antihydra boundary-hitting words (whose drift constant is log2(3/2) as well).

finds.ref: Nicolas Bedaride, Thomas Fernique, "Weak local rules for planar octagonal tilings", 2016/17, Israel J. Math. (arXiv:1512.04679); companion: Th. Fernique, M. Sablik, "Local rules for computable planar tilings" (colored local rules iff computable slope); background: L. Levitov 1988, T.T.Q. Le 1990s
finds.the_theorem: Theorem 1 (Bedaride-Fernique): a 2-plane E in R^4 (slope of a planar octagonal cut-and-project tiling) admits WEAK LOCAL RULES — a radius r and thickness t such that any octagonal tiling all of whose r-patterns occur in some planar tiling of slope E must itself be planar with slope E — if and only if E is determined by its SUBPERIODS (integer linear constraints); corollary: every such slope is defined by quadratic irrationalities (proving Thang Le's 1990s conjecture). Complementary theorem (Fernique-Sablik): with DECORATED tiles, a cut-and-project tiling admits local rules iff its slope is computable (proof simulates Turing machines in the decorations).
finds.the_match: A finite local rule is exactly a 'certificate by finite patterns' that an infinite object has a prescribed irrational slope forever — the tiling-theoretic version of our question 'can a finite automaton certify an invariant set of T avoiding 1?'. The dichotomy is the sharpest known statement of WHERE such certificates live: undecorated/pattern-only certificates exist only for QUADRATIC slopes; decorated (computation-carrying) certificates exist exactly for COMPUTABLE slopes. Our slope-like invariant is log2 3 (or log2(3/2)) — transcendental by Gelfond-Schneider, hence on the wrong side of the algebraic line but trivially computable: the theorem-pair predicts that no pattern/sofic-style certificate class can pin Collatz-extremal itineraries, while computation-embedding certificate classes can (vacuously, by simulation) — locating the certificate frontier exactly between 'algebraic' and 'computable'. Gaps, honestly large: their theorem is for 2D planar rhombus tilings (4->2 projections), not 1D words — the 1D avatar is the classical theorem that a Sturmian word is substitution-invariant iff its slope is a quadratic irrational (Crisp-Moran-Pollington-Shiue 1993 / Yasutomi 1999), and our divergent-orbit parity words are not literally Sturmian (only cycle-extremal words are); no direct formal reduction from Collatz certificates to local rules exists yet. Blindness: full arXiv text checked, zero Collatz/3x+1 mentions.
finds.transfer: Prove a no-go meta-theorem in their style: 'no sofic/finite-pattern constraint system over parity-valuation alphabets can have, as its exact set of admissible infinite configurations, the itineraries of a Collatz-cycle-extremal (mechanical, slope log2(3/2)) family — because pattern-enforceable slopes are quadratic and log2(3/2) is transcendental (Gelfond-Schneider)'; concretely, adapt the subperiod obstruction: compute the 'subperiods' (rational linear relations) of the Collatz itinerary constraint system and show they do NOT determine the slope, which by the BF criterion rules out weak-local-rule-type certificates of every radius — a scale-free strengthening of our 7-state DFA negative result, for the cycle-extremal subclass.

finds.ref: Jorg Endrullis, Hans Zantema, "Proving non-termination by finite automata", 2015, RTA 2015 (LIPIcs 36), 160-176; adjacent: A. Geser, D. Hofbauer, J. Waldmann, "Match-bounded string rewriting systems", 2004, AAECC 15, 149-171; D. Hofbauer, J. Waldmann, "Sparse tiling through overlap closures for termination of string rewriting", 2020 (arXiv:2003.01696)
finds.the_theorem: Endrullis-Zantema: if there is a non-empty regular (tree) language L closed under rewriting by a left-linear TRS R and containing no normal forms, then R is non-terminating; closure is certified by Genet's criterion (Lemma 14: for each rule l->r, every state-assignment run l-alpha =>* q implies r-alpha =>* q), the whole search is encoded as a SAT instance over automata with a FIXED number of states, and a refinement (their Sec. 5) labels automaton states with rule sets, strictly extending the certificate class at equal state count; duality: 'not weakly normalizing' corresponds to strongly closed languages, 'not strongly normalizing' to weakly closed ones. Match-bounds theorem (GHW 2004): match-bounded string rewriting systems preserve regularity of languages, are linearly terminating, match-boundedness for a given bound is decidable, and termination is decidable for inverses of match-bounded systems.
finds.the_match: This is our divergence-certificate framework, independently built and automated in the rewriting community: 'invariant regular set avoiding normal forms' = our 'invariant set of the rational transduction T avoiding 1', their SAT-over-fixed-state-automata search = our exhaustive <=7-state DFA search, and their weak/strong closure dichotomy formalizes exactly the difference between certifying one divergent orbit and certifying a divergent invariant family under the nondeterministic inverse system. Gaps: their languages live on TERM encodings of configurations, and Collatz must first be presented as a left-linear string/term rewriting system (standard encodings exist: unary-with-markers, base-2 transducer rules); their method certifies non-termination of THE REWRITE SYSTEM, which for a Collatz encoding means 'some configuration rewrites forever' — matching divergence only if the encoding makes T-orbits the only infinite reductions (encoding-soundness must be proved per encoding). Blindness: full LIPIcs text checked, zero Collatz/3x+1 mentions (flagship example is the S-combinator; note Zantema separately wrote Collatz-adjacent rewriting papers, but THIS result and paper are blind).
finds.transfer: Two mechanical transfers: (1) re-run our certificate impossibility at the strictly larger class: encode T (and Antihydra) as left-linear string rewriting, then use their exact SAT encoding with rule-labeled states (Sec. 5) and tree (not just string) automata to either find a certificate our DFA search missed or push the negative result to 'no rule-labeled tree-automaton certificate with <= s states', a genuinely stronger statement than our regular-language one; (2) test match-boundedness of the Collatz string system and its inverse — if any Collatz encoding is match-bounded, GHW decidability results would apply to fragments of the problem, and the certified-termination toolchain (Matchbox/CeTA) gives machine-checked certificates for whatever is found.

finds.ref: Mark Goresky, Andrew Klapper, "Efficient multiply-with-carry random number generators with maximal period", 2003, ACM TOMACS 13(4), 310-321; underlying theory: A. Klapper, M. Goresky, "Feedback shift registers, 2-adic span, and combiners with memory", 1997, J. Cryptology 10, 111-147; R. Couture, P. L'Ecuyer 1994/1997
finds.the_theorem: For the multiply-with-carry generator x_n = (a_1 x_{n-1} + ... + a_r x_{n-r} + c_{n-1}) mod b, c_n = floor((...)/b), with connection integer m = a_r b^r - ... - a_0: Thm 2.1: the output is strictly periodic iff the state invariant h lies in [0, m]; Thm 2.2: on periodic states the output is EXACTLY x_{-r+i} = -A(h b^{-i} mod m) mod b, i.e. the b-adic digit stream of a rational -h/m (conjugation to the rotation h -> h b^{-1} on Z/m); Cor 2.3: if m is prime and b a primitive root mod m the period is m-1; Thm 2.4: d-tuple counts across a period vary by at most 1 (near-perfect equidistribution); Thm 3.1: on any strictly periodic state the CARRY c stays in an exact finite window w- < c < w+ forever, and from any other state the generator monotonically enters it. Klapper-Goresky 1997: every eventually periodic binary stream is the 2-adic expansion of a rational p/q; the minimal FCSR generating a stream has size the '2-adic span/complexity', computable from a prefix by a lattice-based rational-approximation algorithm (2-adic Berlekamp-Massey, after de Weger).
finds.the_match: The MWC/FCSR architecture is literally 'iterate multiply-by-a-small-number with carries in base b, output the digit stream' — the same machine as the base-2 transduction view of T; and its master theorem says the periodic locus is EXACTLY the rational (finite 2-adic) locus with the carry confined to an exact window forever — the clean solved analogue of 'parity stream eventually periodic iff orbit rational/cyclic' (Bernstein-Lagarias on our side) plus a genuine drift-vs-window dichotomy (Thm 3.1) of Antihydra shape. Gaps: MWC has a FIXED connection modulus m and linear (carry-bounded) update, so its state space is finite — Collatz's whole difficulty is the unbounded state space; the transfer is therefore at the level of the OUTPUT STREAM invariants (2-adic complexity) and the carry-automaton architecture, not the state dynamics; also the MWC carry window theorem is proved by monotonicity arguments unavailable for the Antihydra counter (which is a functional of parities, not a carry). Blindness: full text checked, zero Collatz/3x+1 mentions.
finds.transfer: Define the 2-adic complexity profile Phi(N) = size of the minimal FCSR (equivalently, minimal q with parity-prefix = 2-adic expansion of some p/q to N digits) of the first N parity bits of a T-orbit or Antihydra orbit, computed by the lattice rational-approximation algorithm. Provable target: a divergent T-orbit's parity stream has Phi(N) -> infinity with an explicit rate forced by the negative log-drift (an eventually-periodic stream contradicts divergence, and LOW 2-adic complexity for infinitely many N forces abnormally good rational 2-adic approximations, feeding linear-forms-in-logs bounds); computable deliverable: run 2-adic Berlekamp-Massey on BB cryptid parity streams — any complexity plateau IS a finite-state-with-carry certificate candidate (an FCSR generating the stream), a certificate class orthogonal to our DFA search since FCSRs carry unbounded counters.

best_single_bet: Mahler 1968 (Z-numbers). It is the same machine: ceil(3g/2) vs Antihydra's floor(3n/2) (mirror conjugate), a break-off algorithm that halts exactly when an exact 2-adic condition fails, and a rigorous golden-ratio counting theorem (T(t)+1 = Fibonacci(t+1) surviving residue classes mod 2^t) standing where our (1/phi)^n Antihydra heuristic stands. First move (one afternoon to first blood, one week to a theorem): write down the parity-itinerary injectivity mod 2^t for n -> floor(3n/2), enumerate the Antihydra-survivor words of length t by the +2/-1 ballot transfer matrix, and replay Mahler's Section 7-8 residue-class argument verbatim to prove '#{seeds <= x whose Antihydra counter survives t steps} <= C x (lambda/2)^t' — the first rigorous density statement behind the cryptid heuristic; then check whether Mahler's G_k/H_k/a_k cascade (iterate (3/2)^{a_k} with 2^{a_k}||H_k, die when H_k is odd) is formally identical to the Space Needle value+v2 dynamics, which it appears to be on its face.
dead_ends: (1) Eppstein's octal-games survey page has no theorem statement and no Collatz mention — Guy-Smith statement had to be recovered from secondary sources; the Gangolli-Plambeck full text is paywalled (Springer redirect blocks WebFetch; Semantic Scholar API rate-limited twice) — blindness was settled via the Crossref reference list instead, modulo one uninspectable citation (Guy UPINT 1981 p.127, flagged in the find). (2) Benford/leading-digit dynamics of orbits: the relevant papers (Kontorovich-Miller school) explicitly treat 3x+1 — not blind, dropped. (3) Skolem/Positivity problems for linear recurrences ('does a sequence hit 0' = counter-hits-boundary shape) — deliberately not pursued: functionally the same community as the excluded PAM-reachability line. (4) 'Mahler's Z-number and 3/2 number systems' (Akiyama et al.) — lands inside excluded beta-numeration, not used. (5) Streaming/communication-complexity of iterated function composition: initial recon surfaced no crisp theorem matching the smell (pointer-chasing bounds are about rounds, not exact eternal side conditions); abandoned early rather than force a loose analogy. (6) Population-dynamics seasonal matrix models: everything sharp reduces to joint spectral radius = excluded switched-systems territory; skipped.
